paper_focus.title: Unprecedented combination of specific tensile strength and ductility achieved in a novel duplex low-density steel
paper_focus.research_objective: To develop a duplex austenite-ferrite low-density steel that significantly improves the specific strength-ductility combination over single-phase austenitic lightweight steels by exploiting ferrite's grain-refinement effect, crack-deflection capability, and contribution to ultralow density.
main_process_variables: Cold rolling reduction from 6 mm to 3 mm (after hot rolling and 1050°C/0.5h solution treatment)
Homogenization at 1200 °C for 2 h before hot rolling
Tensile testing along rolling direction (RD) and transverse direction (TD)
expected_information_axis: microstructure (grain size, δ-ferrite morphology, κ-carbide precipitation, interfaces)
tensile property (YS, UTS, TE, specific UTS, density)
deformation and cracking mechanism (crack initiation at α/γ interfaces, deflection by δ bands, crack blunting)
comparative benchmarks (Ashby map vs. Al alloys, Ti-6Al-4V, other Fe-Mn-Al-C steels)
